Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Adam Hipp To Compel Plaintiff To Use Her True Name Or Dismiss
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ADAM HIPP's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Use Her True Name Or Dismiss

Defendant Adam Hipp's motion to compel plaintiff to use her true name or to dismiss is denied. None of the three reasons proffered by Mr. Hipp provide a sufficient basis to mandate that plaintiff litigate this case using her true name. Due to the nature of her allegations, plaintiff is presumptively permitted to use a fictitious name to preserve her privacy and Mr. Hipp has not made an evidentiary showing to overcome that presumption.

Order signed in open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Continued To Oct-05-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vince Finaldi of Manly, Stewart & Finaldi telephone number 949-252-9990 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Jane SR Doe.
Erik Babcock of The Law Offices of Erik Babcock telephone number 510-452-8400 for Defendant for Adam Hipp.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed and modified its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ADAM HIPP'S DEMURRER TO all three claims alleged by plaintiff in the complaint is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff's use of a fictitious name does render any of her claims deficient. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff was required to obtain permission from the court prior to using a fictitious name, plaintiff has shown good cause to do so and is now granted that permission. There is no basis for Mr. Hipp to use a fictitious name and thus, to the extent that Mr. Hipp seeks leave to do so, that request is denied without prejudice to bringing a motion based on an evidentiary showing. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
